purpose: Handoff summary for the next agent after the automation, analysis, documentation, and live-verification batch.
summary: The workspace now has verified end-to-end headless automation for make fly and make fly_all, platform-aware NMPC solver regeneration, non-notebook analysis generation, expanded run/docs coverage, and a Quarto architecture writeup. The changes were live-tested against PX4 SITL and pushed to the relevant remotes.
completed_work: Added platform-aware NMPC solver guarding/regeneration in src/nmpc_acados_px4, including a solver metadata stamp and Docker-safe source hashing.
Updated root Makefile so NMPC and C++ controller runs rebuild or regenerate as needed before launch.
Added host-side SITL/bridge orchestration and one-command experiment automation in src/workspace_tools/fly_pipeline.py.
Added non-notebook analysis CLI in src/data_analysis/run_analysis.py.
Updated analysis utilities to avoid hard dependency on LaTeX/Jinja2 and to handle aligned NumPy slices safely.
Expanded README.md, sim_commands.md, and hw_commands.md with operational and recovery details.
Added writeup/workspace_structure_summary.qmd to explain the repo structure and run model.
Ignored generated results and log files in .gitignore.

single_run.command: make -C /home/egmc/ws_contraction_px4 fly CONTROLLER=nmpc_cpp TRAJECTORY=hover HOVER_MODE=1 HEADLESS=1 RUN_NAME=acceptance_fly_nmpc_cpp_hover_v3

runtime_issues_found_and_fixed_during_li: fly_pipeline.py originally required host ros2 for readiness; readiness was moved to docker exec inside the controller container.

The automation container originally exited immediately because docker run used detached mode without an interactive shell lifetime; the container startup now uses -it so docker exec targets remain available.
The readiness probe was checking /fmu/out/vehicle_status, but the active PX4 topic is /fmu/out/vehicle_status_v1; this was corrected.
The NMPC solver stamp logic was hashing the wrong package root inside Docker and tried to read /workspace/src/generate_solver.py; source path resolution was fixed.
Analysis initially failed because pandas.to_latex required jinja2; the LaTeX export path was rewritten to be dependency-free.
Analysis initially failed on a read-only NumPy slice during z-axis sign flipping; aligned arrays are now copied before mutation.

root_repo.message: Automate verified fly/fly_all sweeps with CLI analysis and recovery docs

submodules.message: Add platform-aware NMPC solver guard and Docker-safe source hashing
Document ensure_solver workflow for C++ NMPC builds

notes: Generated log files and results are now gitignored.
The Docker image was rebuilt locally during verification to include pandas.

remaining_caveats: run_analysis.py may print benign warnings when a log does not include lookahead_time; this is expected for some controllers.
The Quarto writeup exists as a .qmd source file but was not rendered to HTML or PDF in this batch.
The automation model is host PX4 plus Dockerized controllers, not PX4 itself running inside Docker.
